reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: The demo runs a REAL in-browser zero-shot-image-classification pass: worker.js imports the shared loadPipeline (lib/webai.js) and calls the low-level path (pipe.processor + pipe.tokenizer with padding:'max_length' + pipe.model) to reach logits_per_image, then sigmoid (SigLIP's real independent per-label read-out), a contrast softmax, cosine similarities and a least-squares scale/bias fit. No canned result. Grounded in the actual worker source + the immutable conformance suite, which tests `loads-clean` (console-clean, headless Chrome) and `calls-real-inference` (source pattern: pipeline(|createModelLoader|...). It replicates the pipeline's hypothesis template ('This is a photo of {}') and SigLIP's fixed max_length=64 padding — matching what the real pipeline feeds the model, so the scores are honest; sigmoid probabilities do NOT sum to 1 (the whole point vs CLIP). NOTE: this is a retrospective code+suite-grounded score — no fresh measured latency/screenshot was captured in this backfill pass, hence 4 not 5.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); sigmoid/softmax/cosine/least-squares are cheap array math in the worker after the single forward pass.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = an INDEPENDENT per-label sigmoid probability (a real 'none of the above' is possible), shown as bars, plus a See-inside surface exposing raw logits, the contrast softmax, cosine similarities and the recovered temperature/bias fit. No canvas overlay — DOM bars + data.
rubric.notes: A genuinely faithful SigLIP demo: it reproduces the pipeline's template + max_length padding rather than bypassing them, so the independent sigmoid scores match the real pipeline — the honesty is in matching the real preprocessing.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/siglip-base-patch16-224, linked to its HF card), the real pipeline task (zero-shot-image-classification), backend (WASM — no WebGPU needed), quantisation (q8), download size (~200 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). The worker's loadPipeline call and the createModelLoader model config both use the SAME id/dtype/sizeMB — no invented API surface.

The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model:{modelId:'Xenova/siglip-base-patch16-224', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:200, requiresWebGPU:false}) — no hand-rolled Load button. A valid current on-device model auto-initialises (checking → initialising → ready); only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise. Conformance tests `shared-auto-init-loader` (source: createModelLoader).
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/siglip-base-patch16-224 so the ~200 MB cache is tracked against the never-silently-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/inference state is announced in role=status aria-live regions (2 aria-live=polite regions in the page). Failures surface on the page as a readable `.status.err` message ("Scoring failed: …"), never only in the console; the shared loader exposes Retry/re-download recovery. Runs on the WASM path everywhere — no WebGPU gate. Conformance tests `live-status-region`, `errors-on-the-page`.

Real keyboard-operable controls: a multi-line labels textarea (your own candidate labels), an optional hypothesis-template field, file dropzone (role=button), sample thumbnails (role=button, Enter/Space), a Score button and a Reset-labels button, plus a threshold slider.

No canned/hardcoded output: every visible result is derived from the real worker run — sigmoid/softmax/cosine/scale-bias are all computed from the real logits_per_image tensor. Conformance carries `no-fake-output` (manual-evidenced) and `calls-real-inference`.

The labels textarea and template field are labelled; sample thumbnails have alt text; buttons (Score, Reset labels) have names; status uses role=status aria-live; results are text-labelled DOM bars. Conformance tests `images-have-alt` and `buttons-have-names` (both script assertions over the DOM).
rubric.notes: models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses the shared design system (public/styles.css) with fluid flex/grid, flex-wrap and intrinsic sizing; conformance carries `responsive-desktop` and `responsive-mobile` (no-horizontal-overflow at ≈1280×800 and ≈360×740 DPR3 touch) plus `parity-both-classes`.
rubric.notes: support.desktop/mobile are still needs-review in models.json (awaiting an agent parity verdict); models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); The forward + post-processing run in the worker; the threshold slider re-thresholds cached scores without re-inference. No main-thread inference or unbounded main-thread loop — INP stays low. Conformance carries `inference-off-main-thread` (source) and `inp-stays-low`.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live; every control keyboard-operable with a name
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; keep all controls keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — labelled labels textarea + template input, named Score/Reset buttons, role=status aria-live; results are readable text-labelled bars, not an opaque canvas.

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks keep INP low off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break heavy synchronous processing out of the main thread (or yield) so the browser can handle input and repaint.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the SigLIP forward + sigmoid/cosine/least-squares math run in worker.js off the main thread.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive layout no horizontal overflow fluid grid intrinsic sizing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — fluid flex/grid with wrapping bar rows + a resizable textarea, design-system sizing.

openQuestions: No fresh measured run captured here; a headless run citing a real sigmoid score on a sample label set + latency would let real-inference score 5.
Automated scan flags sub-24px tap targets on both classes — needs manual verification.

followUpGoals.goal: Capture a retained headless run citing real independent sigmoid scores for a sample label set + latency so real-inference can be evidenced at 5.
Verify sub-24px tap targets and enlarge to ≥24px, then flip support to ok.

summary: Retrospective code+conformance-grounded critique for siglip-zero-shot (Xenova/siglip-base-patch16-224, zero-shot-image-classification, q8, ~200 MB, Apache-2.0). Runs the real SigLIP forward off-thread and — faithfully reproducing the pipeline's hypothesis template + max_length=64 padding — exposes INDEPENDENT per-label sigmoid probabilities (with a contrast softmax, cosines and a recovered scale/bias fit) as text-labelled DOM bars. Shared auto-init loader, live status + on-page errors. Honest gaps: no fresh measured run captured here (real-inference 4) and sub-24px tap-target flags keeping support at needs-review.
